title: DrugScore
description:
Aggregated scoring result for a single drug candidate. Combines evidence strength, sensitivity, resistance, and conflict metrics into a composite overall score for ranking purposes.

This schema represents the per-drug output of the DrugRankingEngine pipeline.

drug_name.description: Name of the drug (generic or brand name as used in the evidence base).

overall_score.description: Composite overall score for ranking. Higher values indicate stronger recommendation priority. Computed as a weighted combination of evidence_score, sensitivity, inverse resistance, and inverse conflict_score.

evidence_score.description: Evidence-derived sub-score containing total weighted score, source diversity, highest evidence tier, confidence score, and optional per-source breakdown.

sensitivity.description: Sensitivity score in [0.0, 1.0] reflecting the proportion and strength of evidence indicating the tumour is likely to respond to this drug. Higher values = more sensitive.

resistance.description: Resistance score in [0.0, 1.0] reflecting the proportion and strength of evidence indicating the tumour is resistant to this drug. Higher values = more resistant (penalises overall score).

conflict_score.description: Conflict score in [0.0, 1.0] reflecting the degree of contradictory evidence among sources. Higher values indicate more conflicting evidence (penalises overall score).

rank.description: 1-based overall rank position among all scored drugs. Rank 1 is the highest-priority recommendation.

details.description: Additional detail fields for traceability and debugging. Typically includes item_count, source_count, highest_weight, and source list from the aggregation step.

item_count.description: Total number of evidence items considered for this drug.

source_count.description: Number of distinct evidence sources contributing to this drug's score.

highest_weight.description: Maximum individual weight among all evidence items for this drug.

sources.description: Sorted list of evidence source names that contributed to this drug's scoring.
